svg.title: Brain Tumor Classification System Architecture
svg.desc: End-to-end system architecture showing input MRI scan flowing through preprocessing, EfficientNet-B0 classification, Grad-CAM explainability, Monte Carlo uncertainty, and clinical output stages
svg.text: Brain tumor MRI classification — system architecture

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What MRI input format does the system accept?')
sendPrompt('Why resize to 224x224 for EfficientNet?')
sendPrompt('What is ImageNet normalization and why is it used?')
sendPrompt('What data augmentation was applied during training?')
sendPrompt('What is ImageNet pretraining and transfer learning?')
sendPrompt('What are MBConv blocks in EfficientNet?')
sendPrompt('What is squeeze-and-excitation attention?')
sendPrompt('How does the classifier head work?')

svg.text: AdamW · cosine annealing · cross-entropy loss · 15 epochs

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does Grad-CAM work mathematically?')
sendPrompt('What are the 4 tumor classes the model predicts?')
sendPrompt('How does Monte Carlo Dropout work?')
sendPrompt('What does the Grad-CAM overlay show a doctor?')
sendPrompt('What does the confidence score mean clinically?')
sendPrompt('What does uncertainty mean in a Monte Carlo result?')
sendPrompt('How is the clinical risk flag computed from confidence and uncertainty?')
g.text: Low / moderate / high — based on confidence + uncertainty

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the MRI upload panel look like?')
sendPrompt('What is shown in the results panel?')
sendPrompt('What does the Grad-CAM panel show?')
sendPrompt('What does the uncertainty panel show?')
sendPrompt('How does this system support a radiologist decision?')
g.text: Tumor type · confidence · heatmap · uncertainty · risk flag · reasoning summary
